events.description: The changes to remove these defaults were deliberate as I believe they are incorrect.

details.body:
The changes to remove these defaults were deliberate as I believe they are incorrect. 

The representation of these values in our backend uses timespans https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_git/winfab-RP?path=/src/WRPService/Model/Public/ClusterUpgradePolicyExternal.cs&version=GBdevelop&line=24&lineEnd=25&lineStartColumn=1&lineEndColumn=1&lineStyle=plain&_a=contents
 
And when looking at existing customer templates, I see they use a format like "00:20:00"
https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_g

details.body: How did we distinguish that it was acceptable to use ProxyResource for these resource definitions? The auto converter had used the Legacy.TrackedResourceWithOptionalLocation.

details.body: same comment as above for the defaults restored here

details.body: why omittag and change the tag name from ClusterResource to Cluster?

events.description: These changes were made to maintain full consistency with the original swagger. However, if the orig

details.body: These changes were made to maintain full consistency with the original swagger. However, if the original swagger has issues and better modifications are available, we prefer to use the improved version. I'll revert this part to the main branch version.

events.description: Using ProxyResource allows for customizing tags. The tags property in Legacy.TrackedResourceWithOpti

details.body:
Using ProxyResource allows for customizing tags. The tags property in Legacy.TrackedResourceWithOptionalLocation does not have x-ms-mutability restrictions, whereas the original swagger specification includes such restrictions.
original swagger: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/61738889a51efdb1e04cf929d9a70d043776227f/specification/servicefabric/resource-manager/Microsoft.ServiceFabric/ServiceFabric/preview/2023-11-01-preview/application.json#L1579-L1589

events.description: As mentioned above, this is done to maintain consistency between the generated and original swagger.

details.body: As mentioned above, this is done to maintain consistency between the generated and original swagger. The auto-generated swagger sometimes differs from the original swagger in terms of operation tags.
all default values removed
specs changes LGTM. Lintdiff and breaking changes detected are in line with what's expected. but unsure why Java and Python SDKs are failing validation now.

events.description: This `ProxyResource` conflicts with `Azure.ResourceManager.ProxyResource`. TCGC would report error o

details.body:
This `ProxyResource` conflicts with `Azure.ResourceManager.ProxyResource`. TCGC would report error on this:
`error @azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core/duplicate-client-name: Client name: "ProxyResource" is duplicated in language scope: "java"`

Why do we define this `ProxyResource`?

events.description: In the original Swagger file, ProxyResource model was defined. The ProxyResource that currently rema

details.body: In the original Swagger file, ProxyResource model was defined. The ProxyResource that currently remains is used as a parent class for basic patch models, such as ApplicationResourceUpdate, and also for the creation of some implicit models, such as ClusterCodeVersions.

events.description: Original Swagger doesn't use `ProxyResource` from common types. I think we shouldn't for now.

details.body:
Original Swagger doesn't use `ProxyResource` from common types. I think we shouldn't for now.

According to Chenjie, there's a way to achieve this: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/c020d9996429fe3efc0acda1052f999ef033fbcb/specification/trafficmanager/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network/TrafficManager/Endpoint.tsp#L20-L53

Would you help have a try?

events.description: I modified the ProxyResource in ServiceFabric following the TrafficManager example by adding customA

details.body: I modified the ProxyResource in ServiceFabric following the TrafficManager example by adding customAzureResource. Is this modification the expected result?

events.description: I think the key point here is to get rid of usage of `Azure.ResourceManager.ProxyResource` so that T

events.description: may I know why `VMSizeResource` needs to extend `Azure.ResourceManager.ProxyResource`? original swag

details.body: may I know why `VMSizeResource` needs to extend `Azure.ResourceManager.ProxyResource`? original swagger you showed doesn't..

events.description: Because `VMSizeResource` is a resource model, the original Swagger manually added id, name, and type

details.body:
Because `VMSizeResource` is a resource model, the original Swagger manually added id, name, and type to make it valid. However, TypeSpec doesn't allow this approach - it requires id, name, and type to be encapsulated as a model. 

At the same time, there is no similar resource model structure in the original Swagger that VMSizeResource can inherit from. This leads to only two methods to handle this situation: 
1. Extends from Azure.ResourceManager.ProxyResource. 
2. Introduce a custom resour

events.description: I see. Can we get rid of the inheritance hierarchy? Seems it's still valid (and doesn't even need `@

details.body:
I see. Can we get rid of the inheritance hierarchy? Seems it's still valid (and doesn't even need `@subscriptionResource`). 
See [playground](https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/playground/?options=%7B%22linterRuleSet%22%3A%7B%22extends%22%3A%5B%22%40azure-tools%2Ftypespec-azure-rulesets%2Fresource-manager%22%5D%7D%7D&c=aW1wb3J0ICJAYXp1cmUtdG9vbHMvdHlwZXNwZWMtxhVjb3JlIjsK3yvFFXJlc291cmNlLW1hbmFnZXLMN8grL29wZW5hcGnVHHJlc3QiOwoKdXNpbmcgVHlwZVNwZWMuUmVzdDvHFUHEasQSxWpNxmnIHckySHR0cNEVT3BlbkFQST

events.description: OK, seems this `VMSizeResource` is added along with the tsp migration, so there won't be breaking ch

details.body:
OK, seems this `VMSizeResource` is added along with the tsp migration, so there won't be breaking changes if we rename it.

Just curious, why not just use the user-defined `ProxyResource` instead of that from common types?

events.description: The TypeSpec definition @discriminator("type") is technically incorrect here since there are no subt

details.body: The TypeSpec definition @discriminator("type") is technically incorrect here since there are no subtypes to discriminate between. The model acts as a bag with a type tag, not a polymorphic base class. This pattern should ideally not use @discriminator at all — the type property should be a plain property of type ServicePlacementPolicyType without the @discriminator decorator.

events.description: We probably will go "garbage in, garbage out" if service defines so..

details.body:
We probably will go "garbage in, garbage out" if service defines so..

Java emitter fix at: https://github.com/microsoft/typespec/pull/10080

events.description: When `@discriminator("type")` is removed, it causes the generated swagger to be missing `"discrimina

details.body: When `@discriminator("type")` is removed, it causes the generated swagger to be missing `"discriminator": "type"`, which creates a difference from the original swagger. As for whether this difference is acceptable, you can ask @iliu816 to get more detailed information.

After reading https://swagger.io/docs/specification/v3_0/data-models/inheritance-and-polymorphism/

I believe this definition was originally supposed to follow the pattern of the underlying data layer at 

https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/main/specification/servicefabric/data-plane/ServiceFabric/stable/8.2/servicefabric.json

but it seems it wasn't fully defined. From the service side, we just pass the customer value through. Our concern is whether any SDKs are depend on the di

events.description: remove the clientName, ProxyResource is already etag
Same concern. Not a blocker though.

details.body:
Same concern. Not a blocker though.

For `VMSizeResource`, we now see `VMSizeResource extends ArmProxyResource`, due to the deduplicate of this `ProxyResource` from common, and another `ProxyResource` defined here.

---

For now, to mitigate, Java will add a tspconfig option.

events.description: The commented example response uses a pointer for an embedded (non-pointer) response field (e.g., `C
The commented example response uses `VMSizeResource: &armservicefabric.VMSizeResource{...}`, but `Un

details.body: The commented example response uses `VMSizeResource: &armservicefabric.VMSizeResource{...}`, but `UnsupportedVMSizesClientGetResponse` embeds `VMSizeResource` as a value in `responses.go`. Update the example's commented response shape to match the actual (non-pointer) field type.

events.description: The commented response example uses `Cluster: &armservicefabric.Cluster{...}`, but `ClustersClientCr
The commented response example uses `ServiceResource: &armservicefabric.ServiceResource{...}`, but `

details.body: The commented response example uses `ServiceResource: &armservicefabric.ServiceResource{...}`, but `ServicesClientGetResponse` embeds `ServiceResource` as a value in `responses.go`. Adjust the commented example response to use a non-pointer composite literal so it matches the actual response type.

## Pull request overview

This PR refreshes the `armservicefabric` Resource Manager Go SDK module by regenerating it from the Service Fabric TypeSpec configuration targeting API version `2026-03-01-preview`, moving the module to `v3.0.0-beta.1` and updating generated clients, fakes, examples, and metadata accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Bump module major version to `v3` (beta) and update dependencies (`azcore` et al.) plus generated metadata.
- Regenerate clients/fakes/examples for the `2026-03-01-
waiting for this pr merged: https://github.com/Azure/autorest.go/pull/1912

events.description: `const { apiVersion: _, ...updatedOptions } = ...` introduces a local `_` binding that is never used

details.body:
`const { apiVersion: _, ...updatedOptions } = ...` introduces a local `_` binding that is never used, which will fail builds when `noUnusedLocals` is enabled (common in this repo). Prefer destructuring into a used variable (e.g., `apiVersion`) or avoid creating a binding at all while omitting the property (e.g., pick `apiVersion` into a variable that you actually return/use, and spread the rest into `updatedOptions`).
```suggestion
  const { apiVersion, ...updatedOptions } = {
    ...options,

events.description: `endpointUrl` can include a trailing `/` (e.g., from `getArmEndpoint`), which produces a double slas

details.body:
`endpointUrl` can include a trailing `/` (e.g., from `getArmEndpoint`), which produces a double slash in the default scope (`https://...//.default`). This can break AAD scope matching. Normalize `endpointUrl` (trim trailing slashes) before using it both for `getClient(...)` and when building the default scopes.
```suggestion
/**
 * Normalizes a service endpoint URL by removing trailing slashes.
 *
 * @internal
 */
function normalizeEndpointUrl(endpointUrl: string): string {
  return endpointUrl.

events.description: `endpointUrl` can include a trailing `/` (e.g., from `getArmEndpoint`), which produces a double slas

details.body: `endpointUrl` can include a trailing `/` (e.g., from `getArmEndpoint`), which produces a double slash in the default scope (`https://...//.default`). This can break AAD scope matching. Normalize `endpointUrl` (trim trailing slashes) before using it both for `getClient(...)` and when building the default scopes.

events.description: The sample constructs `ServiceFabricManagementClient` without a `subscriptionId`, while other sample

details.body:
The sample constructs `ServiceFabricManagementClient` without a `subscriptionId`, while other samples in the same folder pass a subscriptionId and the generated REST context factory requires it. This is likely to be a compile/runtime error. Update the sample to pass a `subscriptionId` (even if the specific operation is subscription-less) consistent with the client constructor signature used elsewhere in this PR.
```suggestion
  const subscriptionId =
    process.env["AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID"] || "

events.description: Setting `"lib": []` removes all built-in library type definitions (including `Promise`, `AsyncIterab

details.body:
Setting `"lib": []` removes all built-in library type definitions (including `Promise`, `AsyncIterable`, etc.), which will break TypeScript compilation for these samples. Use an appropriate lib list (e.g., include `ES2023`, and `DOM` only if needed) or remove the `lib` override entirely to allow TypeScript defaults based on `target`.
```suggestion

```

## Pull request overview

This PR appears to be an AutoRest/TypeSpec regeneration for `@azure/arm-servicefabric` targeting API version `2026-03-01-preview` (beta), including new generated REST “api” surface, “classic” convenience layer, updated samples, and API Extractor reports.

**Changes:**
- Added new generated client context + helpers (cloud environment selection, logger) and new operation groups (e.g., `unsupportedVmSizes`).
- Replaced legacy generated artifacts (paging / lro impl and `ope

events.description: The config disables `@azure/eslint-plugin-azure-sdk` rules. In this repo we should avoid turning off

details.body:
The config disables `@azure/eslint-plugin-azure-sdk` rules. In this repo we should avoid turning off Azure SDK lint rules; instead prefer fixing `package.json` (or adjusting generation) to satisfy them, or (at most) downgrade to `"warn"` if there’s a documented repo-wide exception. Please re-enable these rules and address the underlying violations.
```suggestion
      "@azure/azure-sdk/ts-package-json-files-required": "warn",
      "@azure/azure-sdk/ts-package-json-main-is-cjs": "warn",
```

events.description: `logger` is exported but lacks a TSDoc comment. Please add a brief TSDoc describing what the logger

details.body:
`logger` is exported but lacks a TSDoc comment. Please add a brief TSDoc describing what the logger is for and its expected usage (e.g., intended subsystem name and that it integrates with `@azure/logger`).
```suggestion
import { createClientLogger } from "@azure/logger";

/**
 * The default logger for the `arm-servicefabric` client library.
 *
 * Use this logger for diagnostic output from this package. It integrates with
 * `@azure/logger` and uses the `arm-servicefabric` namespace for log filt

- ⏳ **checkenforcer**: CI pipeline checks are still pending (waiting for all checks to succeed). No failed checks detected yet — please wait for the ADO CI pipeline to complete and recheck if any failures appear.

<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: Management Release Assistant, engine: copilot, model: auto, id: 23935837621, workflow_id: mgmt-review, run: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/actions/runs/23935837621

1 tool issue found in the public API surface.

- **`StatelessServiceProperties.minInstancePercentage`** — typed as `Uint8Array` instead of `number`. A percentage value should be a numeric type; this appears to be a generation tool mapping error.

All other checks passed: version alignment (`4.0.0-beta.1` consistent across `package.json`, context file, and `CHANGELOG.md`), no alpha versions, first release is correctly a preview (`-beta.1`), no `_N`/`AutoGenerated` suffix naming issues, no `unknow

🔴 **Tool Issue** — `StatelessServiceProperties.minInstancePercentage`

```ts
minInstancePercentage?: Uint8Array;
```

`minInstancePercentage` represents a percentage value (0–100) in the Service Fabric spec, but it is mapped to `Uint8Array` here. This is a generation tool type-mapping error.

**Fix:** This should be `number` (or `number | null`) rather than `Uint8Array`. Please report this issue in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) and regener
